# Implementation Plan: World Clock Page

**Branch**: `main` | **Date**: May 9, 2026 | **Spec**: [specs/009-world-clock-page/spec.md](../spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/009-world-clock-page/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by `/speckit.plan` command with user guidance on polished design, Material theming, and Angular/Material best practices.

## Summary

The World Clock page displays current times for three global regions (Brazil/Brasilia, UK/London, China/Shanghai) in a single polished, responsive interface. Users select the "World Clock" menu item to access a dedicated feature route. The page updates live via RxJS timer streams, supporting both digital and analog clock format displays while always showing seconds. Design leverages Angular Material theme tokens for consistency and uses standalone component architecture following Angular 21 patterns.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x, Angular 21.x  
**Primary Dependencies**: Angular 21 (standalone components, signals/computed/effects), RxJS (timer streams), Angular Material 21 (theme tokens, component library), Angular CDK (BreakpointObserver for responsive behavior)  
**Storage**: N/A (runtime-generated state only)  
**Testing**: Vitest component/unit tests, Playwright e2e tests, accessible component patterns  
**Target Platform**: Modern web browsers, responsive (mobile to desktop)
**Project Type**: Angular 21 standalone component web app with Material theming  
**Performance Goals**: Route interactive <300ms, clock updates smooth (<16ms frame time for secondhand animation if analog)  
**Constraints**: Strict TypeScript, Material Design System compliance (theme tokens only), WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility, no hardcoded colors (use Material CSS custom properties)  
**Scale/Scope**: Single dedicated page feature (1 route, 3 timezone regions, 2 display format options per timezone)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

✅ **Spec First, Always**: Every implementation slice below maps to one or more requirement/scenario:
- Digital clock display (US2) → FR-002, FR-003, FR-005
- Analog clock display (US2) → FR-002, FR-003, FR-005
- Live time updates (US1, US2) → FR-004
- Responsive layout (US3) → FR-006
- Brazil/UK/China timezones (US1) → FR-002, FR-003
- Menu integration (user guidance) → implicit requirement

✅ **Angular-Idiomatic by Default**: Design uses Angular 21 native patterns:
- Standalone component for world-clock page
- Route composition via app.routes.ts (no lazy loading needed for single page)
- Signals (computed) for reactive clock state
- RxJS timer + interval streams for live updates (proven pattern from feature-007)
- Angular Material component library for UI cards/grid
- BreakpointObserver for responsive breakpoints (pattern from current app)
- Strong type contracts for clock entry and page state

✅ **Strong Typing and Contracts**: Explicit types/contracts needed:
- `WorldClockEntry` interface (region, timezone identifier, current time, format preference)
- `WorldClockPageState` interface (entries collection, loading state, error messaging)
- `ClockFormat` enum (digital | analog)
- Route data contract for world-clock route
- Service interface for timezone + current time resolution
- No untyped JSON or broad assertions

✅ **Test at the Right Level**: 
- Component-level tests for rendering, format switching, responsiveness
- Service-level tests for timezone calculation, timer correctness
- e2e tests for route navigation (menu → page), visible time progression

✅ **Architectural Simplicity**: Single new feature route + dedicated component + simple time-resolution service
- No shared abstractions (keep local to feature)
- Reuses existing Material theming infrastructure
- Reuses existing responsive patterns (BreakpointObserver)
- No added state management layer (component signals sufficient)

**Structure Decision**: Single route within existing feature-based organization. World Clock is a self-contained feature module with internal components, no cross-feature dependencies. Reuses shared Material design system and responsive layout patterns.

## Phase 0: Research & Unknowns Resolution

**Objective**: Identify and resolve unknowns in Technical Context and user guidance.

### Research Tasks

#### Task P0.1: Timezone Precision & Daylight Saving
**Unknown**: How to reliably resolve and maintain current times for Brazil (Brasília, UTC-3), UK (London, UTC±0/+1), China (Shanghai, UTC+8) accounting for daylight saving transitions?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Use JavaScript `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with BCP 47 locale tags (pt-BR, en-GB, zh-CN) and timezone identifiers (America/Sao_Paulo, Europe/London, Asia/Shanghai)
- Rationale: Built-in browser API, no external dependency, automatic DST handling
- Alternatives Considered: moment-timezone (heavier), date-fns-tz (adds dependency)
- Implementation: Create timezone constants as objects `{ region: "Brazil", label: "Brasília", locale: "pt-BR", timeZone: "America/Sao_Paulo" }`

#### Task P0.2: Clock Update Performance
**Unknown**: How to efficiently update three clocks every second without frame drops or unnecessary re-renders?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Use RxJS `interval(1000)` timer stream piped to component signal via `toSignal()`
- Rationale: Proven pattern in feature-007 (digital clock header), efficient subscription cleanup with component destroy
- Alternatives Considered: setInterval (requires manual cleanup), requestAnimationFrame (overkill for 1s tick)
- Implementation: Service returns `Observable<Date>`, component wraps in `toSignal()` with initialValue
- Performance: ~3 component re-renders/sec for second updates, <1ms update latency per clock

#### Task P0.3: Digital vs. Analog Format Toggle & Persistence
**Unknown**: Should format preference be stored per-timezone, globally, or ephemeral per session?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Global format preference per page session (no persistence across page reloads)
- Rationale: Spec scope is "display current times," not session persistence; simpler implementation, aligns with read-only page intent
- Alternatives Considered: localStorage per timezone, per-region settings (adds complexity)
- Implementation: Component signal `currentFormat = signal<ClockFormat>('digital')` toggleable via UI button, one format for all three clocks

#### Task P0.4: Responsive Layout Breakpoints
**Unknown**: How to adapt three-clock layout for mobile/tablet/desktop without truncation?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Use Material BreakpointObserver with 3 responsive modes:
  - Mobile (xs/sm <768px): Stack clocks vertically
  - Tablet (md 768px-1024px): 2 clocks per row
  - Desktop (lg >1024px): 3 clocks per row in horizontal card grid
- Rationale: Consistent with current app patterns (seen in home/account pages), Material Design standard
- Alternatives Considered: CSS Grid auto-fit (less predictable), media queries (manual breakpoint sync)
- Implementation: Signal-based responsive state computed from BreakpointObserver

#### Task P0.5: Polished Design & Material Token Usage
**Unknown**: How to apply "polished design" using Material theme tokens and best practices?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Use Material Design System with:
  - Color tokens: --mat-sys-surface, --mat-sys-on-surface, --mat-sys-surface-container-high, --mat-sys-primary, --mat-sys-on-primary
  - Typography tokens: --mat-sys-title-medium, --mat-sys-body-medium, --mat-sys-body-small
  - Spacing: clamp() for responsive padding (seen in feature-008 refactoring)
  - Cards: Material mat-card component with elevation + spacing
  - Fonts: Roboto (Material default via theme)
- Rationale: Proven in features 007-008, ensures design consistency, automatic theme switching support
- Alternatives Considered: Custom CSS (breaks theme switching), Material lite (insufficient components)
- Implementation: No hardcoded colors; all color/spacing via CSS custom properties

#### Task P0.6: Accessibility & WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
**Unknown**: What accessibility patterns ensure world-clock page meets WCAG 2.1 AA?

**Resolution**:
- Decision: Implement:
  - Semantic HTML (role="region", aria-label for each clock)
  - ARIA live regions for dynamic time updates (aria-live="polite" on clock display)
  - Keyboard navigation: Tab through clocks, format toggle via space/enter
  - Color contrast: 4.5:1 min for labels/times (Material tokens enforce this)
  - Focus indicators: visible focus ring from Material theme
- Rationale: Aligns with current app accessibility practices (seen in home/account tests)
- Alternatives Considered: None—WCAG 2.1 AA is project requirement
- Implementation: Component template uses semantic structure + aria-labels; tests verify contrast + focus

### Research Summary

All unknowns resolved. Technical approach confirmed viable and consistent with project patterns.
